name: Shakti Spear
description: The Shakti Spear, also known as the Vel in Tamil, is a divine spear associated primarily with the Hindu god Kartikeya (also known as Skanda or Murugan), the god of war and victory. It is a symbol of power, courage, and divine energy. The Shakti Spear is not merely a weapon; it represents the concentrated power of the goddess Shakti, the divine feminine energy and the source of all creation. It embodies her strength, wisdom, and unwavering resolve. The origin of the Shakti Spear is often linked to Parvati, the consort of Shiva, who bestowed it upon Kartikeya to vanquish the demon Surapadman. The spear possesses immense power, capable of piercing through illusions and destroying evil forces. It is believed to grant victory in battle and protect its wielder from harm. The spear's power is amplified by Kartikeya's divine status as a son of Shiva and Parvati, making it an unstoppable force against injustice. It is revered as a sacred artifact, representing the triumph of good over evil and the embodiment of divine power. The spear is often depicted as a long, slender weapon, sometimes adorned with intricate carvings and precious stones. Its presence inspires faith and courage in devotees, reminding them of the power of righteousness and the ultimate victory of good over evil. The Shakti Spear continues to be a powerful symbol in Hindu tradition, particularly in South India, where Kartikeya is widely worshipped.
subtitle: Divine spear of courage and victory
material: Divine Energy, possibly metal (gold or celestial iron)
origin: Bestowed upon Kartikeya by Parvati to defeat Surapadman.
currentLocation: Worshipped in temples, often depicted in Kartikeya's hand.

keyMyths.title: Defeat of Surapadman
keyMyths.description: Kartikeya used the Shakti Spear to defeat the demon Surapadman, who had terrorized the gods and the world. The spear pierced through Surapadman's illusions and ultimately vanquished him.

keyMyths.title: Gift from Parvati
keyMyths.description: Parvati, recognizing the need for a powerful weapon to combat evil, infused her divine energy into the creation of the Shakti Spear and presented it to her son, Kartikeya. This act symbolized her blessing and empowerment of Kartikeya's divine mission.

sources.author: G.S. Ghurye
sources.description: Provides context on the symbolism of weapons in Indian culture.
sources.text: Provides context on the symbolism of weapons in Indian culture.
